recapPrompt.prompt: Which lesson needs prev, current, and nextNode?

choices.label: Reverse a Linked List
choices.feedback: Correct. Those three pointers make reversal safe.

choices.label: Traverse a Linked List
choices.feedback: Not this one. Traversal usually only needs current.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Explain why the head pointer matters so much in linked lists.

practiceChallenge.title: Mastery check: rebuild the pointer helper
practiceChallenge.description: Complete the helper so it returns the key linked-list safety ideas without the walkthrough.
hints: Null marks the end of the list.
nextNode protects the rest of the list during reversal.
practiceChallenge.successMessage: Pass. You recalled the key linked-list safety rules.
practiceChallenge.retryMessage: Needs practice. Recheck what marks the end, what starts the list, and what protects the forward chain.

blanks.label: Value that marks the end of the list
Pointer at the front of the list
Pointer that protects the rest of the list

visibleTests.label: End marker
visibleTests.scenario: What tells us the list is over?

visibleTests.label: Front pointer
visibleTests.scenario: What points to the front of the list?

visibleTests.label: Saved forward pointer
visibleTests.scenario: What saves the rest of the list during reversal?
